title: Butter Naan
excerpt: Soft, fluffy butter naan made at home, brushed with melted butter and a hint of coriander.

steps: Mix the yeast, sugar and 60 ml lukewarm water in a cup.  Leave it for 10 min until it starts to froth. (forms bubbles)
In a mixing bowl stir the flour and salt together.
Make a well in the flour and mix in the yeast mixture with a wooden spoon.
Now stir the yoghurt, oil and milk into the flour. Stir until everything comes together nicely.

Sprinkle flour onto a surface and knead the dough for 5 minutes until everything comes together nicely.  Put the dough in a bowl and cover it with clingwrap.  Leave it in a warm place until it has doubled.
Divide the dough into 12 portions.  Roll small balls and roll each ball out into a circle.
In a hot pan with a little bit of oil in it, fry the naan for 1 to 2 minutes on a side.
As the naan comes out of the pan, brush it with melted butter.
If you like, you can melt finely chopped garlic together with the butter and brush garlic butter over the naan.  I stirred coriander into my butter.
